title: How He Would Drink His Wine.
content:
Fill me my wine in crystal; thus, and thus
    I see't in's puris naturalibus:
    Unmix'd. I love to have it smirk and shine;
    'Tis sin I know, 'tis sin to throttle wine.
    What madman's he, that when it sparkles so,
    Will cool his flames or quench his fires with snow?
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "How He Would Drink His Wine.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
